nl_prompt: What was total gross revenue last quarter?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(line_value) AS total_gross_revenue
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: How many orders were placed this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(id) AS order_count
FROM orders
WHERE YEAR(order_date) = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is our average order value?
generated_sql:
SELECT AVG(line_value) AS avg_order_value
FROM orders

nl_prompt: How many unique customers made a purchase in the last 6 months?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS unique_customer_count
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 6 MONTH)

	date_sub(VARCHAR, TIMESTAMP, TIMESTAMP) -> BIGINT
	date_sub(VARCHAR, TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) -> BIGINT


LINE 3: WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 6 MONTH)
                            ^"}

nl_prompt: What is the total number of products in our catalog?
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS product_count FROM products

nl_prompt: What was our highest single-order revenue ever?
generated_sql:
SELECT MAX(line_value) AS max_single_order_revenue
FROM orders

nl_prompt: How many orders were cancelled this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(id) AS cancelled_order_count
FROM orders
WHERE YEAR(order_date) = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE) AND status = 'cancelled'

nl_prompt: What is our total revenue for Electronics products?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(line_value) AS total_revenue_electronics
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
WHERE products.category = 'Electronics'

nl_prompt: What is the revenue for the top-selling product?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(line_value) AS total_revenue_top_selling_product, p.name AS product_name
FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.name
ORDER BY SUM(o.line_value) DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: How many new customers signed up this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS new_customer_count
FROM customers
WHERE YEAR(created_at) = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is our total cost of goods sold this year?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(products.cost_price * orders.quantity) AS total_cogs_this_year
FROM orders
JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What percentage of orders were returned?
generated_sql:
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'returned' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) AS return_rate
FROM orders

nl_prompt: How many distinct product categories do we sell?
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT category) AS product_category_count FROM products

nl_prompt: What was our total revenue last year?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(line_value) AS total_revenue_last_year
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND order_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)

nl_prompt: What is the revenue breakdown by product category?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.category, SUM(o.line_value) AS total_revenue_by_category
FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.category

nl_prompt: Which customers placed more than 5 orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.name
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.name
HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 5

nl_prompt: Who are our top 10 customers by lifetime revenue?
generated_sql:
SELECT c.name AS customer_name, SUM(o.line_value) AS total_revenue
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10

FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
LEFT JOIN logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
GROUP BY p.category

nl_prompt: Which product categories have more than 10 percent return rate?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.category, COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'returned' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS return_count
FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.category
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'returned' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) > (SELECT SUM(line_value) * 0.10 FROM orders)

nl_prompt: What is the revenue per customer segment?
generated_sql:
SELECT c.segment, SUM(o.line_value) AS total_revenue_per_segment
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.segment

nl_prompt: Which sales rep closed the most orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT s.rep_name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count
FROM sales_reps s
JOIN orders o ON s.id = o.sales_rep_id
GROUP BY s.rep_name
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: What is the monthly revenue trend for the last 12 months?
generated_sql:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, SUM(line_value) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date)
ORDER BY month

nl_prompt: Which products had zero orders in the last 60 days?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.id, p.name
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id
WHERE oi.order_id IS NULL AND o.date > DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 60 DAY)
GROUP BY p.id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(o.id) = 0

nl_prompt: What is the average order value per customer segment?
generated_sql:
SELECT c.segment, AVG(o.line_value) AS avg_order_value_per_segment
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.segment

nl_prompt: What is the revenue from completed orders versus pending orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN line_value ELSE 0 END) AS completed_revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'pending' THEN line_value ELSE 0 END) AS pending_revenue
FROM 
    orders

nl_prompt: Which product has the highest total quantity sold?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.name AS product_name, SUM(o.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold
FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.name
ORDER BY SUM(o.quantity) DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: What is the total discount given across all orders?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(discount_pct * line_value) AS total_discount_given
FROM orders

nl_prompt: What is the average number of items per order by category?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.category, AVG(oi.quantity) AS avg_items_per_order
FROM order_items oi
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.category

nl_prompt: What percentage of revenue comes from our top 20 percent of customers?
generated_sql:
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN c.id IN (
    SELECT customer_id FROM orders
    ORDER BY line_value DESC
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.20 FROM orders)
)) / SUM(o.line_value)) * 100 AS revenue_percentage_top_20_percent
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id

nl_prompt: Which customers with active CRM deals had delivery issues?
generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.name AS customer_name, COUNT(d.opp_id) AS deal_count
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_reps s ON c.id = s.customer_id
JOIN orders o ON s.id = o.sales_rep_id
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
LEFT JOIN sf_opportunities d ON c.email = sf_accounts.email_address AND sf_accounts.account_id = d.account_id
WHERE d.opp_id IS NOT NULL AND (r.return_date IS NOT NULL OR o.status = 'cancelled')
GROUP BY c.name, deal_count
HAVING COUNT(d.opp_id) > 0

                                    ^'}

nl_prompt: Which of our top 10 revenue customers have an open CRM opportunity?
generated_sql:
SELECT c.name AS customer_name, SUM(o.line_value) AS total_revenue
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN sf_opportunities so ON c.email_address = so.account_owner
WHERE so.stage != 'Closed'
GROUP BY c.name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: What is the delivery success rate for Enterprise segment customers?
generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN d.status_code = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100 / COUNT(*) AS delivery_success_rate
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
JOIN logistics_deliveries d ON o.shipping_id = d.shipping_ref
WHERE c.segment = 'Enterprise'

nl_prompt: Total revenue from customers whose CRM deals closed successfully?
generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(o.line_value) AS total_revenue_successful_deals
FROM orders o
JOIN sf_opportunities so ON o.id = so.opp_id
WHERE so.stage = 'Closed Won'

nl_prompt: Which customers have both high order value and a delayed shipment?
generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.name
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
LEFT JOIN logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
WHERE o.line_value > 1000 AND ld.delivery_date IS NULL

nl_prompt: What is the on-time delivery rate by CRM deal stage?

SELECT 
    s.stage, 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ld.delivery_date >= o.expected_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100 / COUNT(*) AS on_time_delivery_rate
FROM 
    orders o
JOIN 
    sf_opportunities s ON o.customer_id = s.account_id
LEFT JOIN 
    logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
GROUP BY 
    s.stage

nl_prompt: Which product categories have delivery complaints from high-value CRM accounts?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.category, COUNT(CASE WHEN ld.status_code > 200 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS complaint_count
FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
JOIN logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
JOIN sf_accounts sa ON c.email_address = sa.email_address
WHERE p.list_price > 500 AND ld.status_code > 200
GROUP BY p.category
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN ld.status_code > 200 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) > 0

FROM sf_opportunities T1
LEFT JOIN orders T2 ON T1.opp_id = T2.deal_name

nl_prompt: Which logistics carrier has the worst on-time rate for top revenue customers?
generated_sql:
SELECT c.rep_name, AVG(ld.delivery_date - ld.expected_date) AS avg_delay_days
FROM orders o
JOIN sales_reps s ON o.sales_rep_id = s.id
JOIN logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
WHERE o.line_value > 1000
GROUP BY c.rep_name
ORDER BY avg_delay_days DESC
LIMIT 1

nl_prompt: Summarize delivery complaint themes for top 10 revenue customers.
generated_sql:
SELECT c.name AS customer_name, COUNT(r.reason) AS complaint_count
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
WHERE o.line_value > (SELECT AVG(line_value) FROM orders)
GROUP BY c.name
ORDER BY complaint_count DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: Which product categories have the most negative sentiment?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.category, COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'returned' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS return_count
FROM orders o
JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.category
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'returned' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) > (SELECT SUM(line_value) * 0.10 FROM orders)

nl_prompt: What common complaints appear for delayed shipments?
generated_sql:
SELECT r.reason, COUNT(r.id) AS complaint_count
FROM orders o
JOIN logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
WHERE ld.delivery_date IS NULL AND r.reason IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY r.reason
ORDER BY complaint_count DESC

nl_prompt: Find customers who mentioned competitors in support.
generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.name AS customer_name
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
WHERE o.status LIKE '%competitors%'

nl_prompt: What were top 3 reasons for returns?
generated_sql:
SELECT reason, COUNT(*) AS return_count
FROM returns
GROUP BY reason
ORDER BY return_count DESC
LIMIT 3

nl_prompt: Which sales reps have customers with most positive feedback?
generated_sql:
SELECT s.rep_name, COUNT(c.id) AS customer_count
FROM sales_reps s
JOIN orders o ON s.id = o.sales_rep_id
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
WHERE c.segment = 'positive'
GROUP BY s.rep_name
ORDER BY customer_count DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: Summarize key themes for Enterprise segment accounts.
generated_sql:
SELECT c.name AS account_name, COUNT(r.reason) AS complaint_count
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
WHERE c.segment = 'Enterprise' AND o.line_value > (SELECT AVG(line_value) FROM orders)
GROUP BY c.name
ORDER BY complaint_count DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: Which logistics partners have most damaged goods complaints?
generated_sql:
SELECT ld.carrier_name, COUNT(r.id) AS complaint_count
FROM orders o
JOIN logistics_deliveries ld ON o.shipping_id = ld.shipping_ref
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
WHERE r.reason LIKE '%damaged%'
GROUP BY ld.carrier_name
ORDER BY complaint_count DESC
LIMIT 10

nl_prompt: What do churned customers say were top reasons for leaving?
generated_sql:
SELECT c.name AS customer_name, r.reason AS reason
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'cancelled'
GROUP BY c.name, r.reason
ORDER BY COUNT(r.reason) DESC

nl_prompt: Find orders where complaints mention product defects.
generated_sql:
SELECT o.id AS order_id, c.name AS customer_name, r.reason AS complaint_reason
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
LEFT JOIN returns r ON o.id = r.order_id
WHERE r.reason LIKE '%product defect%'
ORDER BY o.order_date DESC
